leadText: Splishy Splash (Japanese: ざぶざぶサーフ Splashing Surf) is a damage-dealing Water-type move introduced during Generation VII, in Pokémon: Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee!. It is one of the signature moves of the partner Pikachu.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] Generation VII [ edit | edit source ] Splishy Splash deals damage and has a 30% chance of paralyzing the target. Generation VIII onwards [ edit | edit source ] Splishy Splash cannot be selected in a battle.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description P E The user charges a huge wave with electricity and hits the opposing Pokémon with the wave. This may also leave the opposing Pokémon with paralysis. Sw Sh BD SP LA S V This move can't be used. It's recommended that this move is forgotten. Once forgotten, this move can't be remembered.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Splishy Splash is the only Water-type move that can inflict paralysis . Splishy Splash may be a reference to Surfing Pikachu .
